text:
Market today: The mood is cautiously optimistic for tech/chips, driven by signals of a dovish Fed and continued strong interest in the semiconductor sector, highlighted by new leveraged ETFs and positive analyst calls on big tech like Microsoft.

Your holdings: SOXL (-35.46%) and SPAL (-37.26%) are your biggest headaches, both leveraged ETFs deep in the red and prone to decay. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny, illiquid spec also down. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) for any further decay or stop breaches. Also, monitor Microsoft's momentum given the positive analyst praise.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged, underperforming positions while selectively looking for high-conviction opportunities in strong tech names.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at 200 is far above the current price. Given the deep loss and leveraged decay, consider adjusting your stop or trimming if it continues to fall.
SPAL: This new, leveraged position is already down -37.26%. Review your stop at 21.38 and consider trimming if it cannot recover, given its volatile nature.
MSFT: With positive news and a scanner setup, consider a new buy at entry 395.21-394.75 with a stop at 393.06 if looking to add tech exposure.
